A Family Proposal · July

Come to
Lésvos

Three days on a Greek island. Diana and I have already been. Here's what's waiting for you.

Diana-Approved  ·  Already Field-Tested

Why Lesvos

The island everyone overlooks.
That's the point.

Lesvos is the third-largest Greek island and one of the best-kept secrets in the Aegean. It's not Santorini — overrun, overpriced, a sunset on a postcard. It's not Mykonos. It's the real Greece: fishing boats at dawn, ouzo at dusk, olive groves that produce more oil than any other island in the country, and food that makes you question every dining decision you've made before.

Diana and I were there July 9–16. We know what to order, where to swim, and when to leave for the hike. This is the trip we'd like to share.

"Lesvos produces half the world's ouzo. The island has its priorities exactly right."

Lesvos, unofficially

The Plan

Three days.
Zero filler.

Below is a full itinerary. Not everything has to happen in this order, and not everything has to happen at all. But this is roughly how we'd propose to spend 72 hours together.

1

Day One

Arrive, Swim, Eat Well

🌊
First swim

The Aegean is 26°C in July. The water around Lesvos is unusually clear and unusually calm. Drop your bags and get in.

🍽️
Dinner at Afros — on the bay

A waterfront restaurant at the Afros hotel — fresh grilled fish, mezedes, local wine, and the bay directly in front of you. Great food, genuinely affordable. Diana and I ate here: you will over-order and not regret it.

🍦
La Casa del Gelato — Molyvos

Ranked #1 for dessert in Molyvos. An Italian-style gelato lab in the heart of the village: homemade flavors made fresh daily with a proper cattabriga machine, €3 per scoop. The pistachio is exceptional. The walk back along the harbour afterward rounds things off nicely.

2

Day Two

Ancient Wonders & Deep Restoration

🌿
The Petrified Forest — UNESCO Global Geopark

Twenty million years ago, volcanic eruptions buried a subtropical forest in ash and pyroclastic flows. What remains is one of the two largest petrified forests in the world — fossilized trunks up to 20 metres long, root systems preserved intact, branches, fruits, and leaves all turned to stone. The site covers 150 km² near Sigri on the west coast and holds the thickest standing petrified tree on Earth (circumference 13.7 metres). The Natural History Museum in Sigri provides excellent context before you walk the site.

♨️
The Eressian Hammam & Spa — Eressos, West Coast

A 2021 winner of "Small Hotel Spa of the Year" in Greece. Featured in Condé Nast Traveller UK. Recommended by the Michelin Hotel Guide. Housed in a restored 1850s mansion in Eressos — the birthplace village of the poet Sappho — the Eressian combines traditional hammam ritual with a full spa menu. Book your session in advance; it fills up.

🏖️
Skala Eressos beach — afternoon

A long Blue Flag sandy beach steps from the Hammam. Good promenade bars and a natural wind-down for the afternoon.

3

Day Three

Adventures & Human Connection

🥾
Mount Olympus Hike — early morning

The trail is exposed and offers little shade — a proper mountain hike, not a Sunday walk. We go early to beat the heat. The view from the top is what you'd expect from a place called Olympus.

⚠️  Start by 7am. Bring 2 litres of water per person. Sun hat and sunscreen are essential.
⛵
Boat rental — with or without a captain

Small motorboats are available to rent independently — anchor in a cove nobody can reach by land, swim off the side, eat lunch on the water. Or hire a captain and let someone else navigate. Seeing the coastline from the water is one of the best things you can do here.

🛶
The Boat Refugee Foundation

Lesvos sits just a few miles from the Turkish coast and has been one of Europe's primary arrival points for people crossing by boat since 2015. The Boat Refugee Foundation — a Dutch humanitarian organization — has been operating here continuously, providing medical care and psychosocial support at the Mavrovouni camp. Spending time with their staff and understanding what the island carries alongside its beauty is something we'd like to share with you.

Everywhere You Look

The beaches.

Lesvos has beaches the way other islands have olive trees. Water quality across the island is officially rated as excellent. Organised or secluded, sandy or pebbled, calm or sporty — these are the highlights.

Vatera Beach

Over 8 km of golden sand on the southern coast — the longest beach on the island. Calm water and waterfront tavernas. A full day sorted.

Molyvos Beach

A pebbly bay beneath a medieval castle, with a village of colourful houses tumbling down to the water. Cobbled streets above, cold beer below. (Yes, that's the view.)

Eftalou Beach

Natural thermal springs at the shoreline north of Molyvos. Warm spring water meets the cool Aegean — a genuinely odd and wonderful combination.

Petra Beach

Golden sand beneath a village where the church sits atop a 25-metre rock, reached by a spiral staircase carved into the cliff. Good promenade cafes.

Agios Isidoros

Ranked in the top ten most beautiful beaches in Greece. A curved arc of white sand and shingle leading to turquoise water near Plomari.

Skala Eressos

Long, sandy, Blue Flag certified, and laid-back. On the west coast near the Hammam. Sunset faces west over open sea.

The Ask

Come join us.

It's July. The Aegean is warm, the evenings are long, and the food is good. We'll be there. Hope you will be too.

Diana & Simmy
